cue: How do you exclaim oh my goodness?

es.meaning: Good heavens; oh my — surprise, dismay, awe.
es.regionNote: Peninsular-leaning; Mexico deploys the madre-family differently and richly (¡a toda madre!, me vale madre, un desmadre) with register hazards everywhere. Reviewer to advise how much of that minefield to flag.

it.meaning: Good heavens; oh my — surprise, dismay, awe.

bridge.note: The rare exact twin that is also a trap of degree: both languages cry out to their mother, same words, same order, same occasions. But Spanish madre carries a second, volatile life — in Mexico the madre-family ranges from high praise (a toda madre) to vulgarity (me vale madre), so the innocent twin sits at the door of a register minefield Italian mamma never enters.

interference.es_to_it: Mamma mia transfers perfectly — say madre mía and be understood everywhere. The danger is the NEXT step: improvising with madre in Mexico without knowing the family.
interference.it_to_es: Madre mía transfers cleanly to mamma mia. Italian mamma is safe in every construction; no minefield on that side.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian Matters marker list 2026-07-20 (discovery source only; all examples freshly authored). ES pairing by Claude, UNVERIFIED. Freebie twin with a register footnote that earns its keep.
